mission.title: lima: v1.0 roadmap: change the default mount driver from reverse-sshfs to 9p (for QEMU) and virtiofs (for vz)
mission.description: v1.0 roadmap: change the default mount driver from reverse-sshfs to 9p (for QEMU) and virtiofs (for vz). Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current lima setup
steps.description:
Verify your lima version and configuration:
```bash
limactl --version
```
This feature requires a working lima installation.

steps.title: Review lima configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant lima configuration:
https://github.com/lima-vm/lima/blob/master/docs/mount.md

Lima v1.0 will change the default mount driver from reverse-sshfs to 9p for QEMU, virtiofs for vz.

steps.title: Apply the fix for v1.0 roadmap: change the default mount driver from…
steps.description:
The `mapped-xattr` model is known to be incompatible with symlinks: https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/issues/173

The `none` model is obviously incompatible with chown, but this is fine, as the reverse-sshfs does not support chown either.

See issue #971
```yaml
nerdctl run --rm -v /Users/$(whoami):/mnt --user 100 alpine cat /mnt/.bashrc
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "v1.0 roadmap: change the default mount driver from…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
The `mapped-xattr` model is known to be incompatible with symlinks: https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/issues/173

The `none` model is obviously incompatible with chown, but this is fine, as the reverse-sshfs does not support chown either.

See issue #971
codeSnippets:
# attempt uid 100, failed
$ nerdctl -- run --rm -v /Users/$(whoami):/mnt --user 100 alpine cat /mnt/.bashrc
cat: can't open '/mnt/.bashrc': Permission denied
FATA[0000] exit status 1

# attempt uid 0, success
$ nerdctl -- run --rm -v /Users/$(whoami):/mnt alpine cat /mnt/.bashrc
[ -f ~/.fzf.bash ] && source ~/.fzf.bash

prerequisites.description: A working lima installation or development environment.
